title: IREN: A Renewable-Powered Data Center Play Positioned for the AI Supercycle
text:
IREN (formerly Iris Energy Limited) has evolved from a Bitcoin mining company to a renewable-powered data center operator positioned for the AI infrastructure supercycle.

Key Business Model: IREN combines vertically integrated operations with access to large quantities of grid-connected renewable energy. The company operates across North America, Europe, and the Asia-Pacific region, leveraging previously developed Bitcoin mining infrastructure for AI compute deployment.

Competitive Positioning: The company's strategic advantage stems from its accumulated portfolio of 4.5+ gigawatts of secured grid-connected power across multiple geographies. This power availability becomes increasingly valuable as the AI industry faces a critical constraint: not semiconductor chips, but reliable electrical power.

Recent Business Milestones: IREN has secured major contracts with Microsoft ($9.7 billion over five years) and NVIDIA ($3.4 billion over five years) for AI cloud services. The company projects reaching $3.4-$3.7 billion in annualized recurring revenue by end of 2026.

Risk Factors: The company faces significant capital requirements, execution risks in transitioning from a mature mining business to nascent AI services, and competitive pressures from established data center operators and other AI infrastructure providers.
